title: thymelaea tarton-raira subvar. valentina
intro: Thymelaea tarton-raira subvar. Valentina, also known as the Patrón or Patrón herb, is a plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small white flowers and shrubby growth. Traditionally, the plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. The health benefits of Thymelaea tarton-raira subvar. Valentina include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, with the dried flowers being used to make a medicinal tea, which can be consumed to take advantage of its health benefits.
